preamble: The transformation of a labio-velar glide [w] to a voiceless velar stop [k] involves fortition and devoicing. This shift is sometimes observed in the dialectal adaptation of loanwords or in specific phonetic environments where glides are realized as stops.

examples.note: Adaptation of glides as voiceless stops in specific phonetic environments.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Philology.
Watson, J. C. (2002). The Phonology and Morphology of Arabic.
